questions.question: Explain the parametric form of a line used in Liang-Barsky algorithm and state the range of the parameter u.
keyPoints: Line is expressed as x = x1 + u(x2-x1) and y = y1 + u(y2-y1)
Parameter u ranges from 0 to 1
At u=0, we get start point; at u=1, we get end point

questions.question: What are the four clipping edges considered in Liang-Barsky algorithm and how are p and q values defined for each?
keyPoints: Four edges: Left(x=xmin), Right(x=xmax), Bottom(y=ymin), Top(y=ymax)
p1=-dx, p2=dx, p3=-dy, p4=dy for LEFT, RIGHT, BOTTOM, TOP
q1=x1-xmin, q2=xmax-x1, q3=y1-ymin, q4=ymax-y1

questions.question: State the conditions for a line to be completely outside the clipping window in Liang-Barsky algorithm.
keyPoints: When p=0 (line parallel to edge) and q<0 (start point outside that edge)
If this condition is true for any edge, the entire line is rejected
This represents trivial rejection case

questions.question: Describe the update rules for parameters u1 and u2 in Liang-Barsky algorithm.
keyPoints: For p < 0 (entering edge): u1 = max(u1, q/p)
For p > 0 (exiting edge): u2 = min(u2, q/p)
Initially u1 = 0 and u2 = 1

questions.question: Compare the computational efficiency of Liang-Barsky algorithm with Cohen-Sutherland algorithm.
keyPoints: Liang-Barsky requires approximately 10 comparisons per line
Cohen-Sutherland may require 15-20 endpoint comparisons
Liang-Barsky has O(1) time complexity and is faster for parametric clipping

questions.question: What is the condition for a valid clipped line in Liang-Barsky algorithm? How are the new endpoints calculated?
keyPoints: Valid when u1 ≤ u2 after processing all four edges
New endpoints: x1' = x1 + u1*(x2-x1), y1' = y1 + u1*(y2-y1)

questions.question: Explain what happens when p=0 for all four clipping edges in Liang-Barsky algorithm.
keyPoints: p=0 means line is parallel to all clipping edges
This occurs when dx=0 and dy=0 (degenerate line) or line is horizontal/vertical and coincident with edges
This represents trivial acceptance case - line is completely inside the window
